# Team News: Belgium vs. Iran injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Jonathan O'Shea| 20 Jun 2026 20:00, Last updated: 20 Jun 2026 20:00

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Both top seeds Belgium and outsiders Iran will aim to seize the initiative in tightly-packed Group G when they resume their World Cup 2026 campaign on Sunday.

With all four teams locked on one point, it could be a crucial contest in Los Angeles, but both sides have lingering injury concerns.

Iran were without their captain Alireza Jahanbakhsh last time out, while Belgium are still missing a first-choice defender.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both nations.

---

## BELGIUM vs. IRAN

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/belgium/world-cup-2026/team-news/GAME%20PAGE%20URL%20HERE

## BELGIUM

Out: Zeno Debast(thigh)

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Courtois; Meunier, Ngoy, Mechele, De Cuyper; Onana, Tielemans; Trossard, De Bruyne, Doku; De Ketelaere

## IRAN

Out: None

Doubtful: Alireza Jahanbakhsh (mu

news_items.title: Belgium XI vs Iran: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard
news_items.author: Oli Gent
news_items.text:
# Belgium XI vs Iran: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard

Author: Oli Gent
Published: 2026-06-19T22:24:42Z
Source: Evening Standard (standard.co.uk)
Language: en

## Story

Belgium XI vs Iran: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026 | The Standard

Sport| Football

# Belgium XI vs Iran: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026

Red Devils started their World Cup campaign unconvincingly

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Belgium take on Iran in the second of their World Cup group games, looking to build on their 1-1 draw with Egypt.

The Red Devils were fortunate to earn a point against Mohamed Salah and co. in their opening match, and Rudi Garcia will be hoping for a better performance this time around against a weaker side.

Formerly the top-ranked side in the FIFA standings, Belgium have undergone a real change in personnel from the ‘golden generation’, but some stars do remain, and others have emerged in the old guard’s places.

Jeremy Doku has taken over on the left wing from Eden Hazard, while Kevin de Bruyne now sports the capta

news_items.title: Jeremy Doku ruled out of Belgium clash with Iran after respiratory infection flares up again | Goal.com
news_items.author: Moataz Bellah El Hadedy
news_items.text:
Jeremy Doku ruled out of Belgium clash with Iran after respiratory infection flares up again | Goal.com

### DON'T MISS A MOMENT OF THE WORLD CUP

Your all-access pass to scores, live updates, and insights Explore

AFP

Jun 20, 2026 21:28+02:00

Belgium’s World Cup preparations have been hit by a significant setback as star winger Jeremy Doku has been ruled out of the upcoming group stage fixture against Iran. The Manchester City attacker is struggling with a recurring respiratory infection that has forced him out of training in Los Angeles.

## A recurring health setback in Los Angeles

The news came as a bolt from the blue for the Red Devils just before their scheduled group training session in California. Doku was a notable absentee from the pitch as the squad gathered to prepare for their second match of the tournament. It has since been confirmed that he will play no part in the clash against Iran due to a persistent respiratory issue.

According to Sporza, the winger has been struggling with his breathing for several weeks. Doku originally travelled with the squad from Belgium while suffering from a severe cold, although he appeared to be on the mend after a strong second-hal

news_items.title: Roozbeh Cheshmi Injury: Won't face Belgium | RotoWire
news_items.text:
Roozbeh Cheshmi Injury: Won't face Belgium | RotoWire

# Roozbeh Cheshmi Injury: Won't face Belgium

Written by RotoWire Staff

Published on June 19, 2026

Cheshmi (hamstring) is out for Sunday's match against Belgium, according to Iran Focus.

Cheshmi is not going to be an option for Iran in their second group stage match of the World Cup, as the defender will now miss both games to open the tournament. This is unfortunate for the team, as he remains an expected starter when fit. This will now leave his availability for the group stage finale in question, possibly not featuring this World Cup at all if they are eliminated.

Free Agent

By Jonathan O'Shea| 20 Jun 2026 20:00, Last updated: 20 Jun 2026 20:00

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Both top seeds Belgium and outsiders Iran will aim to seize the initiative in tightly-packed Group G when they resume their World Cup 2026 campaign on Sunday.

With all four teams locked on one point, it could be a crucial contest in Los Angeles, but both sides have lingering injury concerns.

Iran were without their captain Alireza Jahanbakhsh last time out, while Belgium are still missing a first-choice defender.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both nations.

# Belgium XI vs Iran: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup and injury latest for World Cup 2026

Red Devils started their World Cup campaign unconvincingly

Oli Gent, Sports Reporter@oli_gent

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

Belgium take on Iran in the second of their World Cup group games, looking to build on their 1-1 draw with Egypt.

The Red Devils were fortunate to earn a point against Mohamed Salah and co. in their opening match, and Rudi Garcia will be hoping for a better performance this time around against a weaker side.

Formerly the top-ranked side in the FIFA standings, Belgium have undergone a real change in personnel from the ‘golden generation’, but some stars do remain, and others have emerged in the old guard’s places.

Jeremy Doku has taken over on the left wing from Eden Hazard, while Kevin de Bruyne now sports the capta"}

AFP

Jun 20, 2026 21:28+02:00

Belgium’s World Cup preparations have been hit by a significant setback as star winger Jeremy Doku has been ruled out of the upcoming group stage fixture against Iran. The Manchester City attacker is struggling with a recurring respiratory infection that has forced him out of training in Los Angeles.

## A recurring health setback in Los Angeles

The news came as a bolt from the blue for the Red Devils just before their scheduled group training session in California. Doku was a notable absentee from the pitch as the squad gathered to prepare for their second match of the tournament. It has since been confirmed that he will play no part in the clash against Iran due to a persistent respiratory issue.

According to Sporza, the winger has been struggling with his breathing for several weeks. Doku originally travelled with the squad from Belgium while suffering from a severe cold, although he appeared to be on the mend after a strong second-hal"}

# Roozbeh Cheshmi Injury: Won't face Belgium

Written by RotoWire Staff

Published on June 19, 2026

Cheshmi (hamstring) is out for Sunday's match against Belgium, according to Iran Focus.

Cheshmi is not going to be an option for Iran in their second group stage match of the World Cup, as the defender will now miss both games to open the tournament. This is unfortunate for the team, as he remains an expected starter when fit. This will now leave his availability for the group stage finale in question, possibly not featuring this World Cup at all if they are eliminated.
